slides.title: The number that *predicts how long* you live.
slides.sub: It's called VO2 max — one of the strongest predictors of all-cause mortality we have. Most people never test it.

slides.title: Your engine size
slides.body: VO2 max is how much oxygen your body can actually *use under effort* — basically the size of your aerobic engine.

slides.title: It tracks with lifespan
slides.body: People in the *bottom 25%* of fitness tend to die younger — in some studies the gap rivals smoking.

slides.title: Add hard intervals
slides.body: Once or twice a week, push to *near-max for short bursts* — this is what actually lifts the ceiling.

slides.title: Four by four minutes
slides.body: A classic: *four hard 4-minute efforts* with easy recovery between. Brutal, short, effective.

slides.title: More years, more energy
slides.body: A higher VO2 max means everyday life feels *easier for longer* — stairs, hikes, and decades you'd otherwise lose.

slides.title: Watch your *fitness number* climb.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your workouts, steps, and recovery into one daily score — so you can see your fitness trending up week over week.

caption:
VO2 max is one of the strongest predictors of how long you'll live. ❤️‍🔥

Add a couple hard intervals a week — like four 4-minute efforts — and you raise the ceiling. More years, more everyday energy. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your training into one daily score you can watch climb → link in bio.
youtube_title: VO2 Max: The Number That Predicts How Long You Live
